# Style Reference Guide

<!-- Generated by design-style-agent -->

## Typography

- **h1** — Page titles (Dashboard, Payment Management, Payments Made). Use `text-2xl font-bold` on desktop, `text-xl font-bold` on mobile.
- **h2** — Section headings within a page (e.g., "Agency Summary", "Main Grid", "Parked Payments"). Use `text-lg font-semibold`.
- **h3** — Card titles and chart labels. Use `text-base font-medium`.
- **Body text** — Default `text-sm` (14px). Use for table cells, form labels, and general content.
- **Captions/labels** — `text-xs text-muted-foreground` for secondary information (timestamps, helper text).
- **Monetary values** — Use `font-mono` or `tabular-nums` for currency figures to ensure column alignment in tables. Always format as en-ZA: R 1 234 567,89.
- **Font weights** — `font-bold` for page titles and key metrics only. `font-semibold` for section headings. `font-medium` for card titles and table headers. `font-normal` for everything else.

## Spacing

- **Page padding** — `p-6` on desktop, `p-4` on tablet/mobile.
- **Section gaps** — `space-y-6` between major page sections (charts row, agency grid, etc.).
- **Card padding** — `p-4` internal padding for all Card components.
- **Card gaps** — `gap-4` between cards in a grid layout.
- **Table cell padding** — `px-3 py-2` for standard table cells. `px-3 py-3` for header cells.
- **Form field spacing** — `space-y-4` between form fields. `space-x-2` between inline elements (search bar + filter button).
- **Modal content** — `p-6` padding inside Dialog content areas. `space-y-4` between modal sections.
- **Button spacing** — `gap-2` between adjacent action buttons. Use `ml-auto` for right-aligned primary actions.

## Component Selection

- **Card** — Use for dashboard metric displays (Total Value Ready, Total Value Parked, etc.) and chart containers. Each chart or summary metric gets its own Card.
- **Table** — Use Shadcn `<Table>` for all data grids (Agency Summary, Main Grid, Parked Grid, Payments Made). Never use plain HTML tables.
- **Dialog** — Use for all confirmation modals (park, unpark, initiate payment). Keep modal content focused: a summary of the action plus Confirm/Cancel buttons.
- **Button** — Primary variant (`bg-primary`) for main actions (Initiate Payment, Confirm). Secondary variant for supporting actions (Park Selected, Unpark Selected). Destructive variant only for irreversible actions. Ghost variant for row-level actions (individual Park/Unpark buttons in tables).
- **Checkbox** — Use for multi-select in table rows (bulk park/unpark). Align left in the first column.
- **Input + Select** — Use for search/filter bars. Place in a horizontal flex row above the table they filter.
- **Badge** — Use for payment status indicators (READY, PARKED, PROCESSED). Use colour-coded variants: teal/secondary for READY, amber/warning for PARKED, green/success for PROCESSED.
- **Skeleton** — Use as loading placeholders for cards, charts, and table rows while API data is loading.
- **Tabs** — Do NOT use tabs for the three main screens. Use top-level navigation (nav bar or sidebar) instead, since each screen has its own route.

## Layout

- **Max content width** — `max-w-7xl mx-auto` (1280px) for the main content area. Full-width sidebar if used.
- **Navigation** — Horizontal top nav bar with the MortgageMax logo on the left and navigation links (Dashboard, Payment Management, Payments Made) centered or right-aligned. Auth info (username, sign-out) at the far right.
- **Dashboard layout** — Top row: 2-3 metric cards in a grid (`grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3`). Middle row: charts in a 2-column grid (`grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2`). Bottom: full-width Agency Summary table.
- **Payment Management layout** — Filter bar at top. Main Grid table below. Parked Grid table below Main Grid (always visible, even when empty). Action buttons (Park Selected, Initiate Payment) above or alongside the Main Grid.
- **Payments Made layout** — Filter bar at top. Single full-width table below.
- **Responsive breakpoints** — Mobile (768px min): single column, cards stack vertically, tables scroll horizontally. Tablet (768px–1279px): 2-column card grids, tables still may scroll. Desktop (1280px+): full layout as described above.
- **Table overflow** — Wrap tables in a horizontal scroll container (`overflow-x-auto`) on smaller viewports.

## Motion & Animation

- **Transitions** — Use `transition-colors duration-150` for hover states on buttons, table rows, and cards. Keep animations subtle and fast.
- **Modal enter/exit** — Use the existing `modalEnter`/`modalExit` keyframes in globals.css for Dialog open/close.
- **Loading states** — Use Skeleton components with a subtle pulse animation (Shadcn default). No custom loading spinners needed.
- **Reduced motion** — The `prefers-reduced-motion` media query in globals.css already disables animations. No additional handling needed.
- **Page transitions** — None. Navigation between screens is a standard page load (Next.js App Router handles this).

## Accessibility

- **Contrast** — The dark navy primary (#1a2744 equivalent, oklch 0.25 0.06 255) on white background provides >12:1 contrast ratio. Teal secondary on white provides ~4.7:1 — use only for large text or decorative elements, not body text. For text on teal backgrounds, use white (oklch 0.98+).
- **Focus rings** — Teal focus ring (`ring-ring`) provides strong visibility on both light and dark backgrounds. Do not suppress or hide focus indicators.
- **Table headers** — Always use `<th>` elements with `scope="col"`. Include `aria-sort` on sortable columns if sorting is implemented.
- **Buttons** — All buttons must have visible text labels. Icon-only buttons must have `aria-label`. Disabled buttons (e.g., empty-grid Initiate Payment) use `disabled` attribute, not just visual styling.
- **Modals** — Shadcn Dialog handles focus trapping and `aria-modal` automatically. Ensure modal titles use `DialogTitle` for screen readers.
- **Keyboard navigation** — Tab order follows visual layout. Table row selection (checkboxes) must be keyboard-accessible. Escape closes modals.

## Brand Tone

- **Microcopy** — Professional but approachable. Use clear, direct language. "Process payments" not "Execute batch payment operation."
- **Button labels** — Action-oriented: "Park", "Unpark", "Initiate Payment", "Download Invoice", "Confirm", "Cancel". Avoid vague labels like "Submit" or "OK".
- **Error messages** — Specific and helpful: "Could not park payment — the server returned an error. Try again or contact support." Not "An error occurred."
- **Empty states** — Informative: "No parked payments for this agency" with a subtle icon. Not just a blank area.
- **Confirmation modals** — Summarise the action clearly: "Park 3 payments totalling R 45 678,90?" with Confirm and Cancel buttons.
- **Success feedback** — Brief toast or inline message: "Payment batch processed successfully." Dismiss automatically after 5 seconds.
- **Loading text** — Use skeleton loaders, not "Loading..." text. The visual placeholder communicates loading state without words.
